name: Mozarabic Way: Alboloduy Loop
description: This difficult 24.5-mile touring cycling route from Alhabia offers panoramic views of the Alpujarra Almeriense and cultural landmarks.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alhabia traverse a diverse landscape, from fertile river valleys to arid, desert-like terrain. The region is situated at the confluence of the Nacimiento and Andarax rivers, offering routes that follow these waterways. Cyclists can also access the foothills of the Sierra de Los Filabres, providing varied gradients and elevations. The area features a mix of cultivated lands, sparse vegetation, and unique desert scenery. Best touring cycling routes around Alhabia The most popular touring cycling route is Mozarabic Way: Alboloduy Loop, a 24.5 miles (39.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views of the Alpujarra Almeriense. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valle del Andarax — circular from Gádor via Alhama de Almería, a difficult 15.5 miles (25.0 km) path. This route follows the Andarax river valley, showcasing a contrast to the more arid surroundings. Local touring cyclists also love the View to Observatorio de Calar Alto – Cerro De La Cruz Huecija loop from Alhama de Almería, a 25.6 miles (41.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Alhabia is defined by river valleys, arid landscapes, and mountain foothills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate valley rides to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Alhabia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alhabia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alhabia?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Alhabia listed on komoot, offering a wide range of options for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Alhabia?
A: The region around Alhabia offers incredibly varied terrain. You can expect routes that follow fertile river valleys, traverse arid, desert-like landscapes, and climb into the foothills of mountain ranges like the Sierra de Los Filabres. This diversity provides options for all skill levels, from gentle valley rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alhabia?
A: Almería, where Alhabia is located, is known for its exceptionally favorable climate, boasting around 320 days of sunshine per year and mild winter temperatures (16-22°C). This makes it an excellent destination for year-round cycling, particularly during spring, autumn, and winter when many other areas might be less appealing.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginner touring cyclists in Alhabia?
A: While many routes in the Alhabia area are rated as difficult due to significant elevation gains, there are a few easier options available. Komoot lists 2 easy and 1 moderate touring cycling routes, providing good starting points for those new to the area or seeking a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are the options for advanced touring cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Advanced touring cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes around Alhabia. The region features significant elevation gains, particularly in the foothills of the Sierra de Los Filabres. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31494987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View to Observatorio de Calar Alto – Cerro De La Cruz Huecija loop from Alhama de Almería&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 41.2 km trail with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, offering a substantial workout and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that explore the Tabernas Desert?
A: Yes, you can experience the unique landscape of Europe's only true desert. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Ravine – Tabernas Village and Desert loop from Gádor&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 133.2 km route that takes you through this captivating arid environment, showcasing its canyons and rock formations.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Alhabia offer diverse natural features. You can explore river valleys, arid desert landscapes, and mountain foothills. Notable natural attractions include the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/635124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Oasis&lt;/a&gt;, extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desert trails&lt;/a&gt;, and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4525156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cautivo&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also provide panoramic views of the Alpujarra Almeriense.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting near Alhabia?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366428682/mozarabic-way-alboloduy-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mozarabic Way: Alboloduy Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 39.5 km circular trail offering panoramic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046189443/valle-del-andarax-circular-from-gador-via-alhama-de-almeria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Andarax — circular from Gádor via Alhama de Almería&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the scenic Andarax river valley.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alhabia?
A: The touring cycling routes around Alhabia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 10 reviews. Over 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain, from river valleys to desert landscapes, and the challenging climbs that offer rewarding views.

Q: Are the roads suitable for touring cyclists, and is traffic an issue?
A: The Almería region is noted for having good quality, often traffic-free roads, which provides an excellent environment for touring cyclists. This allows for a more relaxed and enjoyable experience, especially for those who prefer less congested routes.

Q: Can I find routes that follow the rivers in the area?
A: Yes, Alhabia's location at the confluence of the Nacimiento and Andarax rivers means you can find routes that follow these river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046189443/valle-del-andarax-circular-from-gador-via-alhama-de-almeria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Andarax — circular from Gádor via Alhama de Almería&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, showcasing the contrasting scenery of the fertile riverbanks.
tips: Some sections have recently been freshly asphalted

A few remains from the late Bronze Age (approx. 6th century BC) have been found in the municipality; Roman and Visigothic traces are lacking. It can be assumed that Berbers from the Maghreb were the actual founders of the town in the 8th century. The town was reconquered by the Christians at the end of the 15th century (reconquista), but was briefly given as a fief to the last Emir of Granada, Boabdil. Many Moors from the south of Spain retreated to the remote mountains of the Alpujarras during this period; isolated attempts at final Christianization failed, but after the Moorish uprisings of 1499 to 1501 and 1568 to 1571, the last Muslims were expelled or resettled at the beginning of the 17th century.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult for touring cycling. It requires a very good physical condition due to its mixed terrain and elevation changes. While mostly paved, some segments might be unpaved or challenging, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Alboloduy from Alhabia — circular route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of terrain. Approximately 32.6 km are paved, with an additional 6.81 km of paved track. However, be prepared for some sections that might be unpaved, including compact dirt, rocky areas, old mule tracks, and riverbeds. This varied surface is typical of the Alpujarra Almeriense region.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the characteristic beauty of the Alpujarra region, including white-washed villages and terraced hillsides. Notable features include the town of Alboloduy with its traditional architecture, the Church of Saint John Baptist, Saint Christ Hermitage, and the 19th-century Caños Fountain. Don't miss the Beautiful view from viewpoints like the Peñon del Moro, offering panoramic vistas of the river and surrounding areas. The Salty "Balsica" near Alboloduy is also a unique natural feature.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: On average, this touring cycling route takes about 2 hours and 41 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and the specific conditions of the unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Alboloduy from Alhabia — circular route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Alhabia. While specific parking details aren't provided, you can typically find parking in or near the town center of Alhabia, which serves as the trailhead for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification and varied terrain, including potentially unpaved sections, this route is not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires a very good physical condition and experience with touring cycling on mixed surfaces.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the best conditions, consider cycling this route during spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November). The weather is generally milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for a more comfortable and scenic experience.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Alboloduy from Alhabia — circular route?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs on this route, it's important to consider the nature of the trail. As a touring cycling route with mixed terrain and potential road sections, ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling alongside or in a trailer, and always keep them on a leash, especially near roads or in populated areas like Alboloduy.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Alboloduy from Alhabia — circular route. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Camino Mozárabe. Almería-Granada, Carretera Antigua - Río Nacimiento, Vereda de Alcozayar, and the Cordel de la Solana, among others. This offers glimpses into the broader network of trails in the region.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding bike choice for this route?
faqs.answer: While primarily a touring bicycle route, be aware that some sections are unpaved or challenging. A robust touring bike with good tires suitable for mixed surfaces (paved and light gravel/dirt) would be ideal. Road bikes might struggle on the rougher segments, potentially requiring you to dismount and push.
